5)Improved understanding of operator precedence and evaluation.

entries.description:
In the Python session, learned about slicing and understood how it is used to extract specific portions of sequences such as strings, lists, and tuples. Practiced different slicing formats using start, stop, and step values, and observed how slicing allows flexible data manipulation. Covered user input handling and understood how to take input from the user using the input() function and convert it into required data types using type casting.

In the Web Technology session, studied the concept of address and nodes in HTML and understood how elements are structured within a web page. Learned about common abbreviations used in web development and their meanings. Explored the <marquee> tag and understood how it is used to create scrolling text or images on a webpage. Practiced using different attributes of the marquee tag such as direction, behavior, scrollamount, and loop to control its movement and appearance.
entries.learnings:
1)Gained clear understanding of slicing and its syntax.
2)Learned how to handle user input and perform type casting.
3)Developed understanding of HTML node structure.
4)Learned common web development abbreviations.
5)Practiced using marquee tag and its attributes effectively.

entries.description:
In the Python session, learned about the frozenset data type and understood how it differs from a normal set. Studied that a frozenset is immutable, meaning its elements cannot be modified after creation. Explored situations where immutable sets are useful, especially when hashable collection types are required. Covered the dictionary data type and understood how it stores data in key–value pairs. Practiced creating dictionaries, accessing values using keys, updating entries, and deleting elements using built-in methods. Gained clarity on the uniqueness of keys and how dictionaries are commonly used to manage structured data.

In the Web Technology session, gained an introduction to web technology and understood its role in building web applications. Learned how web applications function and how users interact with websites through browsers. Studied the fundamentals of client-server architecture and understood how requests are sent from the client and responses are returned by the server.
entries.learnings:
1)Understood the concept and properties of frozenset.
2)Learned dictionary structure and key–value mapping.
3)Gained practical knowledge of dictionary operations.
4)Developed understanding of web technology fundamentals.
5)Learned the basics of client-server architecture.

entries.description:
In the session, learned about CRUD operations on lists in Python and understood how lists support dynamic data manipulation. Practiced creating lists, accessing elements using indexing, updating values, and deleting elements using built-in methods such as append(), insert(), extend(), remove(), pop(), and clear(). Explored slicing techniques to extract specific portions of a list and observed how lists maintain order and allow duplicate values.
Studied the concept of hashcode and understood how hashing is related to object identity and internal data storage. Gained clarity on the difference between hashable and non-hashable objects and learned why immutable data types like int, string, and tuple can generate hash values, while mutable types like list cannot. This helped in understanding how certain data structures rely on hashing mechanisms.
Covered the set data type and its key characteristics, such as storing unique elements and not supporting indexing. Practiced set methods including add(), update(), remove(), and discard(), and performed operations like union, intersection, difference, and symmetric difference to understand how sets are useful for comparing and filtering data efficiently.
entries.learnings:
1)Gained practical understanding of CRUD operations on lists.
2)Improved knowledge of list methods and indexing techniques.
3)Understood the concept of hashing and hashable objects.
4)Learned the properties and behavior of sets.
5)Strengthened skills in handling collection data types effectively.

entries.description:
In the session, covered other data types in Python and explored the concept of collection data types. Studied types such as list, tuple, set, and dictionary, and understood how they are used to store multiple values. Compared these types based on characteristics like ordering, uniqueness, and ability to modify elements.
Also revised indexing in detail and practiced accessing elements using both positive and negative indexing. Understood how indexing behaves differently depending on the data type, especially in lists and tuples.
Additionally, learned about mutable and immutable objects in Python. Understood that mutable objects such as lists can be modified after creation, whereas immutable objects like tuples and strings cannot be changed once defined. Practiced small examples to observe how modifications work in mutable objects and how immutable objects restrict reassignment of elements.
entries.learnings:
1)Gained understanding of collection data types such as list, tuple, set, and dictionary.
2)Strengthened knowledge of indexing and negative indexing.
3)Learned the difference between mutable and immutable objects.
4)Improved clarity on how different data types behave during modification.

entries.description:
In the session, learned about single value data types in Python and their different types. Studied data types such as int, float, boolean, and complex, and understood that these types store only a single value at a time. Practiced identifying data types and performing basic operations to observe how each type behaves during execution.
Also learned about indexing and how it is used to access individual elements, especially in strings. Understood that indexing starts from 0 and explored the concept of negative indexing to access elements from the end. Practiced small examples to retrieve specific characters using index positions and observed how invalid index values can lead to errors.
Additionally, revised type checking using the type() function and strengthened understanding of how proper knowledge of data types and indexing helps in writing efficient Python programs.
entries.learnings:
1)Gained understanding of single value data types.
2)Learned different types such as int, float, boolean, and complex.
3)Understood indexing and negative indexing.
4)Practiced accessing elements using index positions.
5)Improved clarity on type checking and error handling.

entries.description:
In the session, learned the fundamentals of Python programming and understood its role in full stack development. Focused on understanding how Python programs are written and executed. Explored different data types such as int, float, string, and boolean, and practiced identifying the type of data stored in variables using examples.

Worked on declaring variables, assigning values, and performing basic operations like arithmetic calculations and string handling. Gained clarity on how Python allows variables to store different types of data without explicitly defining the data type. Also learned about variable reinitialization, where the same variable can be assigned a new value during execution, and understood how this affects the stored data.

Practiced writing small code snippets to improve my clarity on these concepts and improve my understanding of variable behavior and data handling in Python.
entries.learnings:
1)Gained understanding of Python basics and its features.
2)Learned different data types in Python.
3)Understood variable declaration and assignment.
4)Practiced variable reinitialization and type change behavior.
5)Developed clarity on dynamic typing in Python.

entries.description:
In the session, gained detailed understanding of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) and its importance in building structured and reliable software systems. Studied the different phases involved in SDLC, including Requirement Analysis, Planning, System Design, Implementation (Coding), Testing, Deployment, and Maintenance. Discussed how each phase contributes to the successful completion of a project and ensures quality, efficiency, and proper documentation.

Also understood how following a systematic development process helps in minimizing errors, improving team coordination, and delivering software that meets user requirements. Real-time examples were discussed to understand how SDLC is applied in industry-level projects.
entries.learnings:
1) Gained clear understanding of all SDLC phases.
2) Understood the importance of structured software development.
3) Learned how documentation and planning impact project success.
4) Developed knowledge about industry-level project workflow.

entries.description:
In the Python session, learned about the while loop and understood how it is used to repeatedly execute a block of code as long as a given condition is true. Studied the basic syntax and flow of execution of a while loop. Practiced writing programs involving number iteration, counting, and condition-based looping. Solved problems such as printing numbers within a specific range, finding sums of numbers, and performing repeated calculations.

Also worked on logic-based examples where the loop runs until a particular condition is met. Understood the importance of updating the loop variable properly to avoid infinite loops. Explored the use of break and continue statements within a while loop to control the flow of execution. This improved understanding of repetition and program control in Python.
entries.learnings:
1)Gained clear understanding of the while loop and its syntax.
2)Learned how condition-based looping works in Python.
3)Practiced solving problems using number iteration and counting logic.
4)Understood how to avoid infinite loops by properly updating variables.
5)Learned the use of break and continue in controlling loop execution.
6)Improved logical thinking and repetition-based problem-solving skills.
